brief_title: Dapagliflozin vs Placebo in Adults with Inadequately Controlled Type 2 Diabetes on Metformin
official_title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 3 Study Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of Dapagliflozin 10mg in Adults with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Inadequately Controlled on Metformin Monotherapy
brief_summary: This Phase 3 randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study evaluates the efficacy and safety of dapagliflozin 10mg added to metformin in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus who have inadequate glycemic control on metformin monotherapy. The primary endpoint is change in HbA1c from baseline at 24 weeks.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Adults aged 18-75 years at screening

2. Diagnosis of type 2 diabetes mellitus for at least 6 months

3. Currently on stable metformin monotherapy (>=1500 mg/day) for at least 12 weeks prior to screening

4. HbA1c between 7.5% and 10.0% (inclusive) at screening

5. Body mass index (BMI) between 25 and 45 kg/m²

6. Estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) >= 45 mL/min/1.73m²

7. Fasting plasma glucose <= 270 mg/dL at screening

8. Willingness to perform self-monitoring of blood glucose

9. Women of childbearing potential must use adequate contraception

10. Able to provide written informed consent
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Type 1 diabetes mellitus or latent autoimmune diabetes in adults (LADA)

2. History of diabetic ketoacidosis

3. Use of any antidiabetic medication other than metformin within 12 weeks of screening

4. eGFR < 45 mL/min/1.73m²

5. History of recurrent urinary tract infections (>= 3 in past year)

6. History of recurrent genital mycotic infections

7. Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic BP > 180 mmHg or diastolic BP > 110 mmHg)

8. New York Heart Association (NYHA) Class IV heart failure

9. Pregnant or breastfeeding women

10. Known hypersensitivity to SGLT2 inhibitors

inclusion_criteria.text: Diagnosis of type 2 diabetes mellitus for at least 6 months
Currently on stable metformin monotherapy (>=1500 mg/day) for at least 12 weeks prior to screening
Body mass index (BMI) between 25 and 45 kg/m²
Fasting plasma glucose <= 270 mg/dL at screening
Willingness to perform self-monitoring of blood glucose
Women of childbearing potential must use adequate contraception
Able to provide written informed consent

exclusion_criteria.text: Type 1 diabetes mellitus or latent autoimmune diabetes in adults (LADA)
Use of any antidiabetic medication other than metformin within 12 weeks of screening
History of recurrent urinary tract infections (>= 3 in past year)
History of recurrent genital mycotic infections
Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic BP > 180 mmHg or diastolic BP > 110 mmHg)
New York Heart Association (NYHA) Class IV heart failure
